title: FPIs pull out Rs 43,000 cr in first week of Jun as AI trade, rupee weakness weigh on Indian equities
description: NEW DELHI, June 7:  Foreign investors continued to pare their exposure to Indian equities, pulling out nearly Rs 43,000 crore in the first week of June amid a shift of global capital towards technology and artificial intelligence (AI)-linked opportunities overseas and persistent weakness in the rupee. With this, total outflows by Foreign Portfolio Investors (FPIs) from Indian equities have reached Rs 2.67 lakh crore so far in 2026, surpassing the Rs 1.66 lakh crore withdrawn during the whole of 2025, […]
content:

title: Audible reveals users have streamed more than 50 million minutes of AI-generated audiobooks

content:

title: The government has no business in AI
description: Whatever gripes people may have with current and future artificial intelligence offerings, the notion of government-run AI poses several major problems.
content: Government cannot keep pace with innovative, cutting-edge tech companies. Government is pretty good at collecting and distributing data and administering programs under well-defined rules, such as sending Social Security checks and averaging hospital... [3693 chars]

title: AI agents now drive more web traffic than humans - is India any different?
description: For the first time, artificial intelligence agents have generated more web traffic than humans globally, according to Cloudflare. This shift, driven by the rapid growth of AI agents, means over 57% of HTTP requests now originate from bots, surpassing human activity.

title: Trump’s kowtow to China on Taiwan risks tech disaster for the U.S. and the world
description: Taiwan produces most of the world’s advanced chips, including for America’s artificial intelligence systems. Bringing even a modest amount of the needed production to the U.S. will take years.
content:
Recently, I attended a showing of a fascinating documentary called A Chip Odyssey that laid out the history of how that small self-governing island democracy of Taiwan came to manufacture around 90% of the world’s most advanced semiconductor chips.
